mission.title: Install and Configure kubeadm (Kubernetes bootstrapper)
mission.description: Kubeadm is a tool designed to simplify the process of creating and managing Kubernetes clusters. It provides a straightforward way to bootstrap a secure cluster and is ideal for users who want to set up a production-grade Kubernetes environment. Key trade-offs include the need for manual configuration of certain components and the requirement for a compatible operating system.

steps.title: Install Kubeadm
steps.description:
This step installs the kubeadm binary on your system, which is necessary for bootstrapping the Kubernetes cluster.

```bash

curl -s https://packages.cloud.google.com/apt/doc/apt-key.gpg | sudo apt-key add -

echo 'deb https://apt.kubernetes.io/ kubernetes-xenial main' | sudo tee /etc/apt/sources.list.d/kubernetes.list

sudo apt-get update

sudo apt-get install -y kubelet=1.31.0-00 kubeadm=1.31.0-00 kubectl=1.31.0-00

sudo apt-mark hold kubelet kubeadm kubectl

```

> Add the Google Cloud public signing key.
> Add the Kubernetes APT repository.
> Update the package list.
> Install specific versions of kubelet, kubeadm, and kubectl.
> Prevent these packages from being automatically updated.
commands: echo 'deb https://apt.kubernetes.io/ kubernetes-xenial main' | sudo tee /etc/apt/sources.list.d/kubernetes.list
sudo apt-get install -y kubelet=1.31.0-00 kubeadm=1.31.0-00 kubectl=1.31.0-00
sudo apt-mark hold kubelet kubeadm kubectl

steps.title: Initialize the Kubernetes Control Plane
steps.description:
This command initializes the Kubernetes control plane on the master node.

```bash

sudo kubeadm init --kubernetes-version v1.31.0 --pod-network-cidr=192.168.0.0/16

```

> Initialize the cluster with a specific Kubernetes version and pod network CIDR.

steps.title: Set Up Kubeconfig
steps.description:
This step configures kubectl to use the new cluster by setting up the kubeconfig file.

```bash

mkdir -p $HOME/.kube

sudo cp -i /etc/kubernetes/admin.conf $HOME/.kube/config

sudo chown $(id -u):$(id -g) $HOME/.kube/config

```

> Create the .kube directory.
> Copy the admin config to the user's kube directory.
> Change ownership of the kube config file.

steps.title: Install a Pod Network Add-on
steps.description:
This step installs a network add-on to allow communication between pods.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://docs.projectcalico.org/manifests/calico.yaml

```

> Install Calico as the network add-on.

steps.title: Verify the Cluster Nodes
steps.description:
This command checks the status of the nodes in the cluster to ensure everything is running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> List all nodes and their statuses.

uninstall.title: Reset the Kubernetes Cluster
uninstall.description:
This command will remove all Kubernetes components and configurations from the host. Data loss may occur, and orphaned resources may remain.

```bash

sudo kubeadm reset

sudo rm -rf $HOME/.kube

```

> Reset the kubeadm configuration.
> Remove the kube config directory.

upgrade.title: Upgrade the Kubernetes Cluster
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, back up your cluster and drain the nodes. This ensures minimal downtime during the upgrade process.

```bash

kubectl drain <node-name> --ignore-daemonsets

sudo kubeadm upgrade plan

sudo kubeadm upgrade apply v1.31.0

kubectl uncordon <node-name>

kubectl get nodes

```

> Drain the node to prepare for upgrade.
> Check available upgrades.
> Upgrade the cluster to the specified version.
> Uncordon the node after upgrade.
> Verify that all nodes are ready.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'kubelet not running'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The kubelet service may not be started or may have failed.

**Fix:**
Run 'sudo systemctl start kubelet' to start the kubelet service.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Unable to connect to the server: x509: certificate signed by unknown authority'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The kubeconfig file may not be correctly configured or the certificates may be invalid.

**Fix:**
Ensure the kubeconfig file is correctly set up and the certificates are valid.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'pod network not ready'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The network add-on may not be installed or configured correctly.

**Fix:**
Reinstall the network add-on using 'kubectl apply -f <network-addon-yaml>'.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'kubeadm init failed'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** Insufficient resources or incompatible OS version.

**Fix:**
Ensure the system meets the minimum requirements and is running a supported OS.

**Affected versions:** <= 1.30

versionNotes.changes: Added support for the new PodSecurity admission controller, improved kubeadm upgrade process.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated the --feature-gates flag for certain features.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from v1.30, ensure to review the new PodSecurity policies.

resolution.summary: You should see the nodes listed with 'Ready' status when running 'kubectl get nodes'.

sudo apt-mark hold kubelet kubeadm kubectl

prerequisites.description: Ensure your system meets the minimum resource requirements and has the necessary tools installed.
